title: O happy day that fixed my choice
firstLine: O happy day that fixed my choice
publicDomainBasis: Philip Doddridge (1702–1751), Edward Francis Rimbault (1816–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
O happy day that fixed my choice
On Thee, my Savior and my God!
Well may this glowing heart rejoice,
And tell its raptures all abroad.

sections.label: Chorus
Chorus
sections.text:
Now rest, my long-divided heart,
Fixed on this blissful center, rest;
Here have I found a nobler part,
Here heav’nly pleasures fill my breast.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
High heav’n that hears the solemn vow,
That vow renewed shall daily hear!
Till in life’s latest hour I bow,
And bless, in death, a bond so dear.
